source.criteria: The mappable proxy that represents the geological features related to the source of MVT Zinc Lead deposits could be the distribution of hydrothermal alteration minerals, such as dolomite, calcite, silicification, and clay, as well as the zoning patterns of these alteration minerals. Geology:Geological terrane:Name of a geological terrane that predominates an H3 cell area. A geological terrane refers to a distinct and relatively large area with a specific geological history and characteristics that differentiate it from surrounding regions. Terranes are often bounded by faults, thrusts, or other geological features, and they can have unique combinations of rocks, structures, and tectonic histories.:Terrane name (raster value with the largest sum of coverage fractions):Terrane_Majority

document.title: A Deposit Model for Mississippi Valley-Type Lead-Zinc Ores

source.criteria:
The mappable proxy that represents the geological features related to the source of MVT Zinc Lead deposits could include:
- Distribution of key minerals and rock types
- Presence of faults
- Petrology of associated igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic rocks
- Fracture density and permeability
- Secondary porosity
- Hydrothermal cement
- Facies transition
- Environment of deposition
- Chemical transport and transfer processes
- Nature of traps and geological controls that trigger ore precipitation

Lithology reclassified to 31 generalized sub-type lithologic groups of igneous, metamorphic, and sedimentary rocks (Lawley and others, 2022). These generalized classifications are based on interpretation of map unit descriptions in the different map compilations. Given that map unit descriptions often contain multiple rock types, there were subjective calls necessary when assigning generalized lithologic classification. :Lithology sub-type (raster value with the largest sum of coverage fraction):Geology_Lithology_Majority

document.title: A Deposit Model for Mississippi Valley-Type Lead-Zinc Ores

source.criteria: The paragraph suggests that a mappable proxy for the source of MVT Zinc Lead deposits could be the distribution of anomalously metal-rich fluids, which can be inferred from water quality data at selected sites in the ore district.

document.title: A Deposit Model for Mississippi Valley-Type Lead-Zinc Ores

source.criteria:
Mappable proxy representing the geological features related to the source of MVT Zinc Lead deposits:
- Presence of dolostone and limestone in platform carbonate sequences
- Location at flanks of basins, orogenic forelands, or foreland thrust belts inboard of clastic rock-dominated passive margin sequences
- No spatial or temporal relation to igneous rocks
- Evidence of ore fluids derived from evaporated seawater and driven within platform carbonates by large-scale tectonic events
- Formation mainly during the Phanerozoic, with the majority hosted in Phanerozoic rocks and formed during Devonian to Permian time, corresponding to intense tectonic events during assimilation of Pangea

pathway.criteria: The mappable proxy that represents the geological features related to the pathway of MVT Zinc Lead deposits could be the distribution and orientation of extensional faults and fractures within the host rocks.
